title: Lepidaria kingii
taxonomy: Lepidaria kingii is a species of flowering plant in the family Asphodelaceae. It is native to the Western Cape province of South Africa. The plant is classified as a succulent and is characterized by its rosettes of fleshy, strap-like leaves. Lepidaria kingii is often found in rocky, mountainous areas and is adapted to survive in dry conditions. The plant is also known by the common name "King's lepidaria" or "King's asphodel".
